payload.answer: Recall selected mem_new because it is the latest non-superseded memory tied to Sam from Goldman. mem_old was filtered out because the system treats superseded memories as historical/inactive and does not present them as current recall results. This preserves the rule that deleted or superseded facts must not be surfaced as if they are still current.
payload.decision: Recall returned mem_new because it is the active memory for Sam associated with Goldman, and filtered mem_old because mem_old is marked superseded by the newer memory.

entity_resolution.reason: The query targets Sam, and both memories refer to the same resolved person entity.

memory_cards.statement: mem_new is the current retained memory for Sam from Goldman.
mem_old is not returned as current recall because it is superseded by mem_new.

details: Applied default recall filter to exclude superseded memory from current results.

repair_options: Inspect the superseded_by link from mem_old to mem_new.
Verify mem_new and mem_old resolve to the same Sam entity and source context (Goldman).
If historical recall is desired, enable inclusion of superseded memories in debug mode only.
Check ranking/filter logic to confirm superseded memories are excluded from default recall.
